mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in real GDP signals a robust economic backdrop that supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. This trend signal is always active and does not require any threshold condition.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is negative in at least 40% of quarters when GDPC1 is above its 4-quarter moving average, or if the correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A material increase in the federal funds rate of 50 basis points or more over a three-month period signals an acceleration in monetary tightening. This increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 50bp over a rolling 3-month window
failure_condition: In periods when the trigger is active, if the average 20-day SPX return is not negative and statistically significant (t-test p < 0.10) compared to non-trigger periods, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling retail sales indicate a divergence where employment growth is not translating into consumer spending. This points to weakening aggregate demand and signals a negative outlook for corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (PAYEMS up, RSAFS down) does not predict negative returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the average return difference between signal and non-signal periods is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession recovery phase, a sustained increase in industrial production reflects improving factory output and supply-side strength, which boosts economic momentum and equity prices over the very short term of 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive but the subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time during the identified regime, the hypothesis is invalidated.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above the 5% threshold, it signals a significant labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day SPX return when UNRATE exceeds 5% is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) relative to the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When rising interest rates coincide with declining housing starts, it signals that monetary tightening is directly suppressing the interest-sensitive housing sector, indicating broader economic weakness that will weigh on equity markets over a quarterly horizon of 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint increase in FEDFUNDS and decrease in HOUST does not lead to negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 65% of occurrences, or if the average return in signal periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle labor market where unemployment is very low (below 4%), further tightening signals overheating and risks of wage inflation prompting aggressive Fed response, which is negative for equity prices over the next 5 trading days as markets price in rate hikes.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day SPX return when UNRATE is below 4% is not negative or is not significantly different from the overall average return (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate a healthy manufacturing sector and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month (20 trading days). This is an always-on trend signal.
failure_condition: If the mean 20-day SPX return following positive INDPRO growth is not greater than that following negative INDPRO growth, with at least 0.5% difference, over a 3-year rolling window.
